questions.q: In which Indian city is the Jama Masjid (the Friday Mosque) located?
questions.explanation: The Jama Masjid stands in Old Delhi, near the Red Fort in the historic walled city.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor commissioned the building of the Jama Masjid in Delhi?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan, the fifth Mughal emperor, ordered the construction of the great mosque.

questions.q: What is the primary purpose of the Jama Masjid?
questions.explanation: It is a congregational mosque (masjid), used especially for Friday prayers.

questions.q: What does the name 'Jama Masjid' essentially refer to?
questions.explanation: 'Jama Masjid' means the congregational mosque, where the community gathers for Friday prayers.

questions.q: Which two materials dominate the construction of the Jama Masjid?
questions.explanation: The mosque is built chiefly of red sandstone with white marble accents, a hallmark of Shah Jahan's architecture.

questions.q: The Jama Masjid is best described as which kind of monument?
questions.explanation: It is one of India's largest mosques and a major Islamic place of worship.

questions.q: How many tall minarets rise at the corners of the Jama Masjid's prayer hall?
questions.explanation: The mosque has two soaring minarets flanking its main prayer hall facade.

questions.q: Roughly in which century was the Jama Masjid in Delhi completed?
questions.explanation: Construction was completed in the mid-17th century, in the year 1656.

questions.q: To which empire does the Jama Masjid in Delhi belong?
questions.explanation: It was built under the Mughal Empire by the emperor Shah Jahan.

questions.q: Which famous Mughal fortress stands near the Jama Masjid in Old Delhi?
questions.explanation: The Jama Masjid sits a short distance from Shah Jahan's Red Fort in Old Delhi.

questions.q: In which year was the Jama Masjid in Delhi completed?
questions.explanation: The mosque was completed in 1656 after about six years of construction.

questions.q: The Jama Masjid was a centrepiece of which new capital city founded by Shah Jahan?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan founded the walled city of Shahjahanabad (today's Old Delhi), of which the mosque was a focal point.

questions.q: How many large domes crown the prayer hall of the Jama Masjid?
questions.explanation: Three bulbous marble domes, striped with black, top the main prayer hall.

questions.q: On a raised platform above what kind of ground does the Jama Masjid stand, giving it prominence over the surrounding city?
questions.explanation: The mosque was built atop a small rocky outcrop, Bhojla Pahari, raising it above the streets of the old city.

questions.q: Approximately how many worshippers can the large courtyard of the Jama Masjid hold?
questions.explanation: The vast courtyard can accommodate roughly 25,000 worshippers at once.

questions.q: How many monumental gateways give access to the courtyard of the Jama Masjid?
questions.explanation: Three grand gateways, reached by flights of steps, lead into the great courtyard.

questions.q: Of what material are the two tall minarets of the Jama Masjid chiefly built, with marble striping?
questions.explanation: The minarets are built of red sandstone with vertical strips of white marble, and they are climbed by interior staircases.

questions.q: Which Mughal noble is traditionally credited with overseeing the construction of the Jama Masjid?
questions.explanation: The project is associated with Shah Jahan's wazir Saadullah Khan and supervisor Fazil Khan during construction from 1650 to 1656.

questions.q: Which architectural feature in the mosque's courtyard is used for ritual washing before prayer?
questions.explanation: A central ablution tank in the courtyard provides water for the ritual washing (wudu) before prayers.

questions.q: Toward which holy city does the prayer hall (qibla wall) of the Jama Masjid orient worshippers?
questions.explanation: Like all mosques, its qibla wall and mihrab orient worshippers toward Mecca.

questions.q: The Jama Masjid in Delhi is best known for being among which category of mosques in India?
questions.explanation: It is counted among the largest mosques in India by capacity and footprint.

questions.q: What is the broad architectural style of the Jama Masjid in Delhi?
questions.explanation: It is a classic example of Mughal Indo-Islamic architecture, with domes, arches, minarets and a vast courtyard.

questions.q: What was the original formal name given to the Jama Masjid by Shah Jahan?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan named it Masjid-i Jahan-Numa, meaning the mosque commanding a view of the world.

questions.q: Approximately how many years did the construction of the Jama Masjid take?
questions.explanation: Construction ran from about 1650 to 1656, roughly six years.

questions.q: How many steps lead up to the grand eastern gateway, once reserved for the emperor's entry?
questions.explanation: Broad flights of about 35 steps rise to the eastern (royal) gateway of the mosque.

questions.q: Approximately how tall are the two minarets of the Jama Masjid?
questions.explanation: Each minaret rises roughly 40 metres (about 130 feet) and can be climbed by an internal staircase.

questions.q: The black-and-white striping on the domes and minarets of the Jama Masjid is created by combining white marble with which dark stone?
questions.explanation: Alternating inlays of white and black marble produce the characteristic vertical striping on the domes and minarets.

questions.q: Which later figure of the 1857 uprising is famously associated with addressing crowds from or near the Jama Masjid?
questions.explanation: The last Mughal emperor Bahadur Shah Zafar is linked to the mosque during the events of the 1857 revolt in Delhi.

questions.q: Why does the Jama Masjid's prayer hall facade place its tallest, deepest archway at the centre?
options: As the grand central iwan (portal) marking the main entrance to the prayer hall
questions.explanation: The large central pishtaq or iwan is a tall recessed portal that emphasises the main entrance to the prayer chamber, a typical Mughal device.

questions.q: Relative to Shah Jahan's other great works such as the Taj Mahal and the Red Fort, when was the Jama Masjid completed?
options: After the Taj Mahal and the Red Fort
At exactly the same time as the Taj Mahal
questions.explanation: The Jama Masjid, finished in 1656, was completed after the Taj Mahal (around 1653) and the Red Fort (1648), late in Shah Jahan's reign.
